records.data_scope: Synthetic defaults used to construct demonstration scenarios
known_limitations: Values are not estimates of real-world platforms, people, markets, or policy outcomes.
Scenario defaults are convenience inputs for synthetic runs.
invalid_uses: Claims of empirical calibration or validation

known_limitations: Replay equality proves reproducibility only.
It is not evidence of empirical validity.
invalid_uses: Evidence that outputs match real systems

records.data_scope: Synthetic classifier scores derived from configured precision/recall assumptions
known_limitations: Operating points are scenario inputs, not measured classifier performance.
Scores are generated from known synthetic categories.

records.data_scope: Classifier trained on synthetic category-token template text during a run
known_limitations: The fitted runtime artifact is not the external benchmark artifact.
LLM-generated text is distribution shift relative to the template training text.

records.source_version_or_revision: bundled gzipped JSONL files, hashed at evaluation time
records.license: CC0-1.0 and Apache-2.0 as recorded per dataset
records.data_scope: Small de-identified text benchmark samples for component tests
records.population: public benchmark text samples, not SocioSim users
records.time_window: source dataset snapshots used to create bundled samples
known_limitations: Applies only to the benchmark evaluation artifact.
Does not validate SocioSim run outputs or runtime template classifier behavior.
valid_uses: Component benchmark evaluation with source hashes and leakage checks

records.source_version_or_revision: legacy summary files without complete source metadata
records.data_scope: Coarse aggregate target values and tolerances
known_limitations: Missing source version, source hash, date range, population, and tolerance rationale for some targets.
Cannot support validation, calibration, or backtest seals.
valid_uses: Synthetic aggregate-fit diagnostic when explicitly labeled unsupported

records.source_title: SocioSim v4 direct-use synthetic decorative assets
records.population: no people; no brands; no platform screenshots
known_limitations: Images are decorative synthetic assets and are not visual evidence.
Human review must be recorded separately before claiming visual review.

records.source_title: Emergence of Scaling in Random Networks (Science 286:509)
records.data_scope: published aggregate statistic (power-law exponent of the actor-collaboration degree tail)
records.population: movie-actor collaboration graph, N=212,250, mean degree 28.78
records.time_window: not stated in the source (pre-1999 corpus)
known_limitations: an actor-collaboration graph is not an online social feed
the paper's exponents span 2.1-4 across networks; the commonly repeated '2-3 for social networks' band does not appear in it
later measurements (Kwak 2010, Ugander 2011) reject a pure power-law for real platform degree distributions
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on a synthetic graph's tail exponent

records.source_title: Measurement and Analysis of Online Social Networks (IMC'07)
records.data_scope: published aggregate statistics (clustering coefficients of four online social networks)
records.population: Flickr, LiveJournal, Orkut, YouTube public user-link crawls (1.1M-5.3M users each)
known_limitations: the paper measures a DIRECTED clustering coefficient; this simulator measures the undirected one, so the definitions are not interchangeable
measured values span 0.136-0.330 across the four networks
the paper's reported degree exponents (1.50-1.99) are all below the conventional 2-3 band and its fit convention is unstated
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on a synthetic graph's clustering

records.source_title: Rhythms of Social Interaction: Messaging Within a Massive Online Network
records.data_scope: published aggregate statistic (hour-of-day messaging peak and trough)
records.population: 4.2 million Facebook users (US college students), 362 million messages and pokes
known_limitations: US college students on 2005-2006 Facebook, not a general or present-day population
measures private messaging rhythm, not public posting volume
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on a synthetic diurnal curve

records.source_title: Pew Research Center — Sizing Up Twitter Users (2019)
records.data_scope: published aggregate statistics (tweet-volume decomposition by user decile)
records.population: 2,791 US adult Twitter users, probability-based panel linked to observed account data
known_limitations: the comparable per-agent MEAN (~0.51/day) is DERIVED from the published decile decomposition, not published directly
the published MEDIAN is 2 tweets/month (~0.066/day): the mean is not a typical user
Pew's 2021 follow-up is materially lower, so the figure is not period-stable
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on synthetic posting volume (as a mean)
invalid_uses: any claim about a median or typical user

records.source_title: Real-Time Bidding Benchmarking with iPinYou Dataset (arXiv:1407.7073)
records.population: 9 advertiser campaigns on the iPinYou DSP real-time-bidding logs
known_limitations: desktop DISPLAY real-time bidding in China, not social-feed advertising (this simulator's ad surface)
no named, publicly verifiable primary source for a social-feed CTR was found; commercial aggregator figures rest on proprietary, non-auditable samples and are deliberately not used
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on synthetic ad click-through
invalid_uses: any claim about social-feed ad performance

records.source_title: YouTube California AB 587 Transparency Report, H1 2024
records.data_scope: published aggregate statistics (appeals filed and reinstatements granted)
records.population: YouTube videos removed for Community Guidelines violations and appealed (745,707 appeals; 82,190 reinstatements)
known_limitations: one platform, one half-year, video removals only
Meta and TikTok omit the numeric tables from their AB 587 filings, so no cross-platform rate is derivable
not period-stable: reporting around Q4 2019 implies roughly double this rate
valid_uses: aggregate-fit diagnostic on a synthetic appeals workflow
invalid_uses: compliance assessment of any real platform
